_description: ISL (Indian Sign Language) reference dataset. Contains descriptions, hand instructions, and reference image URLs for each gesture in the MUDRA catalog.
_source_notes: Descriptions are based on standard ISL conventions. Reference images link to public ISL resources.

A.description: Make a fist with the thumb resting against the side of the index finger. All fingers are curled tightly into the palm.
A.tips: Keep fist tight, thumb pressed flat against the side of the index finger, not wrapped around it.

B.description: Hold all four fingers straight up and pressed together. The thumb is tucked across the palm, touching the base of the fingers.
B.tips: Fingers should be straight and touching each other with no gaps.

C.description: Curve your fingers and thumb to form the shape of the letter C. The hand forms a half-circle or crescent shape.
C.tips: Maintain a smooth curve. Don't let fingers touch the thumb.

D.description: Extend the index finger straight up. Touch the tips of the middle, ring, and pinky fingers to the tip of the thumb, forming a circle at the base.
D.tips: Index finger should be perfectly vertical. Other three fingers touch the thumb tip.

E.description: Curl all four fingers down toward the palm. The thumb is tucked in front of the fingers, across the curled fingertips.
E.tips: Fingers are curled but not in a full fist — fingertips are visible.

F.description: Touch the tip of the index finger to the tip of the thumb, forming a small circle. The middle, ring, and pinky fingers are extended straight up.
F.tips: Three extended fingers should be spread slightly apart.

G.description: Extend the index finger and thumb horizontally, pointing to the side. The other three fingers are curled into the palm.
G.tips: Hand is held sideways. Index finger and thumb are parallel, pointing in the same direction.

H.description: Extend the index and middle fingers together horizontally, pointing to the side. The thumb rests on top of the curled ring and pinky fingers.
H.tips: Two fingers are together and pointing sideways, like a horizontal peace sign.

I.description: Make a fist with only the pinky finger extended straight up.
I.tips: Keep the pinky straight and all other fingers tightly closed.

J.description: Start with the pinky finger extended (like I), then trace a J shape in the air by curving the hand downward.
J.tips: This is a dynamic sign — the motion matters. Start from the I position and curve down and to the left.

K.description: Extend the index and middle fingers in a V shape. The thumb is placed between them, touching the side of the middle finger.
K.tips: The V shape should be wide, with the thumb clearly wedged between the two fingers.

L.description: Extend the index finger straight up and the thumb straight out to the side, forming an L shape. Other fingers are curled in.
L.tips: The angle between thumb and index finger should be approximately 90 degrees.

M.description: Place the thumb under the index, middle, and ring fingers, which are draped over the thumb. The pinky is tucked in.
M.tips: Three fingers rest over the thumb. The thumb tip is visible between the ring and pinky fingers.

N.description: Place the thumb under the index and middle fingers, which drape over the thumb. The ring and pinky fingers are tucked in.
N.tips: Similar to M but only two fingers over the thumb. Thumb tip peeks between the middle and ring fingers.

O.description: Curve all fingers and the thumb to form a round O shape, with fingertips meeting the thumb tip.
O.tips: All fingertips should meet the thumb to form a complete circle.

P.description: Similar to K but with the hand pointing downward. Index and middle fingers are extended in a V, thumb between them, wrist bent down.
P.tips: The key difference from K is the downward orientation of the hand.

Q.description: Similar to G but with the hand pointing downward. Index finger and thumb are extended, pointing toward the ground.
Q.tips: Like G rotated downward. Index finger and thumb point toward the floor.

R.description: Cross the index and middle fingers, with the index finger on top. Other fingers and thumb are curled in.
R.tips: The cross should be tight — middle finger wraps behind the index finger.

S.description: Make a fist with the thumb wrapped across the front of the curled fingers.
S.tips: Different from A — here the thumb is in front of the fingers, not on the side.

T.description: Make a fist with the thumb tucked between the index and middle fingers.
T.tips: The thumb tip should be visible poking out between the index and middle fingers.

U.description: Extend the index and middle fingers straight up, pressed together. Thumb holds down the ring and pinky fingers.
U.tips: The two fingers should be touching each other and pointing straight up.

V.description: Extend the index and middle fingers in a V shape (peace sign). Other fingers are curled in with the thumb holding them.
V.tips: The V should be a clear spread between the two fingers.

W.description: Extend the index, middle, and ring fingers spread apart. The thumb holds the pinky finger down against the palm.
W.tips: Three fingers are spread wide, like the number 3.

X.description: Extend the index finger and bend it into a hook shape. All other fingers are curled into a fist.
X.tips: The index finger should be clearly hooked, not straight and not fully curled.

Y.description: Extend the thumb and pinky finger outward. The index, middle, and ring fingers are curled into the palm.

Z.description: Extend the index finger and trace a Z shape in the air — diagonal down-right, horizontal left, diagonal down-right.
Z.tips: This is a dynamic sign. Draw the Z shape clearly with your index finger.

Namaste.description: Press both palms together in front of your chest with fingers pointing upward. Bow slightly as a sign of respect.
Namaste.tips: Keep palms flat and pressed firmly together. Fingers point upward.

Hello.description: Raise your open right hand near your forehead and wave it outward in a small arc.
Hello.tips: The wave should be a smooth outward motion from near the temple.

Good Morning.description: Combine the sign for 'good' (flat hand moves from chin forward) with 'morning' (arm rises like the sun rising).
Good Morning.tips: The rising motion should be slow and deliberate, mimicking sunrise.

Good Night.description: Combine the sign for 'good' with 'night' (arm descends and hand closes, mimicking sunset and darkness).
Good Night.tips: The descending motion should be smooth with the hand gradually closing.

How Are You.description: Point to the person, then make curved hands at the chest and move them outward with a questioning expression.
How Are You.tips: Facial expression (raised eyebrows) is important for the question form.

I Am Fine.description: Touch your chest with an open hand, then move the hand outward with a thumbs up or positive gesture.
I Am Fine.tips: Start with the hand on the chest, then move it outward confidently.

Thank You.description: Touch your fingertips to your chin or lips, then move your flat hand forward and slightly downward, palm facing up.
Thank You.tips: The forward motion should be graceful and deliberate.

Sorry.description: Make a fist and rub it in a circular motion over your heart/chest area.
Sorry.tips: The circular motion should be slow and show sincerity.

Please.description: Place your flat hand on your chest and move it in a circular motion.
Please.tips: Use an open palm and keep the motion gentle.

Welcome.description: Hold your open hand out, palm up, and sweep it inward toward your body in a welcoming gesture.
Welcome.tips: The sweeping motion should be warm and inviting.

Yes.description: Make a fist and nod it forward, like a head nodding yes. The fist moves up and down.
Yes.tips: A simple fist nodding motion. Keep it clear and definite.

No.description: Extend your index and middle fingers and snap them together with the thumb, like a small pinching or clicking motion.
No.tips: The snapping motion should be quick and decisive.

Okay.description: Touch the tip of the index finger to the thumb, forming a circle. Other fingers are extended.
Okay.tips: Similar to the universal OK gesture.

Help.description: Place a fist on an open palm and raise both hands upward together.
Help.tips: The upward motion shows the request for assistance.

Stop.description: Hold up an open palm facing forward, fingers together and pointing up. The hand is firm and still.
Stop.tips: Keep the palm flat and facing the other person directly.

Go.description: Point the index finger forward and move the hand in the direction of travel.
Go.tips: The pointing motion should be clear and directional.

Come.description: Extend your hand with palm facing up, then curl the fingers inward in a beckoning motion.
Come.tips: The beckoning motion should be repeated 2-3 times.

Wait.description: Hold up both hands, palms facing forward with fingers spread, and hold still.
Wait.tips: Keep hands steady and still — the stillness itself conveys 'wait'.

Finish.description: Hold both hands up with fingers spread, then quickly turn them outward and down.
Finish.tips: The outward flip should be quick and definitive.

Start.description: Place the index finger of the dominant hand between the index and middle fingers of the other hand, then twist.
Start.tips: The twisting motion represents turning a key to start something.

Learn.description: Place fingertips of one hand on your open opposite palm, then bring the fingers up to your forehead.
Learn.tips: The motion from palm to forehead represents taking knowledge into the mind.

Study.description: Hold one hand flat (like a book) and wiggle the fingers of the other hand above it, as if reading.
Study.tips: The wiggling fingers represent eyes scanning a page.

Teacher.description: Touch both hands to the temples, then move them forward and down as if sharing knowledge.
Teacher.tips: The motion represents knowledge flowing from the head outward.

Student.description: Hold one palm open (like a book) and use the other hand to mime taking knowledge from it to the forehead.
Student.tips: Similar to 'learn' but with emphasis on the person receiving knowledge.

Book.description: Hold both hands flat, palms together, then open them like opening a book.
Book.tips: Palms start together and separate, mimicking a book opening.

Pen.description: Mime holding a pen with your dominant hand and make a small writing gesture on the opposite palm.
Pen.tips: The writing motion should be small and recognizable.

Notebook.description: Combine the sign for book (opening palms) with a writing gesture on the opened palm.
Notebook.tips: Open the book first, then write on it.

Exam.description: Make writing motions with both hands simultaneously, as if writing answers on a paper.
Exam.tips: Both hands write at the same time to show urgency of an exam.

Pass.description: Move a flat hand forward past the other hand, indicating moving past a barrier.
Pass.tips: The forward motion represents successfully passing through.

Fail.description: Slide the flat dominant hand off the opposite palm downward, indicating falling down.
Fail.tips: The downward sliding motion represents falling or failing.

Family.description: Make the sign for 'F' with both hands, then circle them forward until the thumbs and index fingers touch.
Family.tips: The circular motion represents the family circle.

Mother.description: Touch the chin with the thumb of an open hand (fingers spread), palm facing left.
Mother.tips: The thumb should clearly touch the chin area.

Father.description: Touch the forehead with the thumb of an open hand (fingers spread), palm facing left.
Father.tips: Like 'mother' but touching the forehead instead of the chin.

Brother.description: Touch the forehead with the thumb (like 'father'), then bring both index fingers together parallel.
Brother.tips: The parallel index fingers represent a same-level family member.

Sister.description: Touch the chin with the thumb (like 'mother'), then bring both index fingers together parallel.
Sister.tips: The parallel index fingers represent a same-level family member.

Baby.description: Cradle your arms as if holding a baby and rock them gently from side to side.
Baby.tips: The rocking motion should be gentle and nurturing.

Friend.description: Hook the index fingers together, then reverse and hook them the other way.
Friend.tips: The hooked fingers represent a bond between two people.

Home.description: Touch your fingertips together to form a triangle or roof shape above your head.
Home.tips: The triangle shape represents the roof of a house.

Marriage.description: Clasp both hands together, interlocking the fingers, representing the joining of two people.
Marriage.tips: The interlocked hands should come together from separate positions.

Child.description: Hold your hand flat, palm facing down, and lower it to indicate the small height of a child.
Child.tips: The lowered hand shows the short stature of a child.

Water.description: Make a 'W' shape with three fingers and tap the chin with the index finger 2-3 times.
Water.tips: The tapping on the chin represents drinking water.

Food.description: Bring your fingertips together and tap them to your lips repeatedly, as if putting food in your mouth.
Food.tips: The tapping should be gentle and represent eating.

Rice.description: Cup both hands together and mime scooping rice from a bowl to your mouth.
Rice.tips: The scooping gesture should be clear and deliberate.

Milk.description: Make a fist and squeeze it open and closed, as if milking a cow.
Milk.tips: The squeezing motion should be repeated 2-3 times.

Tea.description: Mime holding a small cup and sipping from it, pinky slightly extended.
Tea.tips: The sipping gesture with a small cup distinguishes tea from other drinks.

Coffee.description: Place one fist on top of the other and rotate the top fist, as if grinding coffee beans.
Coffee.tips: The grinding motion distinguishes coffee from tea.

Sleep.description: Tilt your head to one side, place your palm against your tilted cheek, and close your eyes.
Sleep.tips: The tilted head and closed eyes make this sign very clear.

Wake Up.description: Place closed fists near your eyes (as if sleeping), then open them wide while opening your eyes.
Wake Up.tips: The transition from closed to open represents waking up.

Bath.description: Rub both fists up and down on your chest, as if scrubbing your body.
Bath.tips: The scrubbing motion should be clear and repeated.

Work.description: Tap the dominant fist on top of the non-dominant fist repeatedly, representing hammering or working.
Work.tips: The tapping represents manual labor or effort.

Shop.description: Mime taking money from a wallet and handing it over, representing a purchase.
Shop.tips: The money exchange gesture makes the meaning clear.

Money.description: Rub your thumb and fingers together repeatedly, representing handling money.
Money.tips: The rubbing motion should be at chest level.

Bus.description: Mime holding a large steering wheel and turning it, representing driving a large vehicle.
Bus.tips: The large steering wheel motion distinguishes bus from car.

Train.description: Extend both arms and move them in a back-and-forth motion like train wheels on tracks.
Train.tips: The rhythmic motion represents the chugging of a train.

Car.description: Mime holding a steering wheel at chest level and make small turning motions.
Car.tips: The steering wheel is smaller than the bus sign.

Bike.description: Make fists and rotate them alternately, as if pedaling a bicycle with your hands.
Bike.tips: The pedaling motion should be circular and alternating.

Road.description: Place both flat hands parallel and move them forward, representing a path stretching ahead.
Road.tips: The parallel hands represent the two sides of a road.

Left.description: Point or sweep your flat hand to the left side.
Left.tips: A clear directional motion to the left.

Right.description: Point or sweep your flat hand to the right side.
Right.tips: A clear directional motion to the right.

Straight.description: Hold your flat hand vertically and push it straight forward.
Straight.tips: The forward push should be direct and unwavering.

Near.description: Hold both hands apart, then bring them close together without touching.
Near.tips: The close proximity of the hands represents nearness.

Far.description: Hold both hands together, then move them apart to a wide distance.

Hospital.description: Use the index finger to draw a cross (+) shape on the upper arm, representing the medical cross.
Hospital.tips: The cross should be drawn clearly on the upper arm or shoulder area.

Doctor.description: Touch the wrist of one hand with the fingers of the other, as if checking a pulse.
Doctor.tips: The pulse-checking gesture clearly identifies a doctor.

Medicine.description: Mime pouring medicine into the palm of one hand and taking it to the mouth.
Medicine.tips: The pouring and taking motion should be clear.

Pain.description: Point both index fingers toward each other near the affected area and twist them, representing sharp pain.
Pain.tips: The twisting motion represents the sensation of pain.

Blood.description: Touch the back of one hand, then wiggle fingers downward, representing liquid flowing.
Blood.tips: The downward wiggling fingers represent blood dripping.

Emergency.description: Wave both hands rapidly in the air with urgency, or make the sign for 'help' with rapid motion.
Emergency.tips: Speed and urgency distinguish this from regular signs.

Police.description: Tap the area above the chest (where a badge would be) with the flat hand or make a C shape there.
Police.tips: The badge-tapping gesture is the key identifier.

Fire.description: Hold both hands with fingers spread, palms facing you, and wiggle all fingers while moving hands upward, representing flames.
Fire.tips: The upward wiggling motion represents dancing flames.

Danger.description: Hold up one fist and thrust it forward forcefully with a warning expression.
Danger.tips: The facial expression is important — show concern/warning.

Call.description: Hold your hand in a Y-shape (thumb and pinky extended) near your ear, like a phone.
Call.tips: The phone gesture is universally understood.

Time.description: Tap your wrist (where a watch would be) with the index finger of the other hand.
Time.tips: The watch-tapping gesture clearly means time.

Day.description: Hold one arm horizontal and bring the dominant hand up from behind it in an arc, like the sun rising and crossing the sky.
Day.tips: The arc represents the sun's path during the day.

Night.description: Hold one arm horizontal and bring the dominant hand down in a curve behind it, like the sun setting.
Night.tips: The downward curve represents the sun going below the horizon.

Today.description: Point downward with both hands, palms down, indicating 'now' or 'this day'.
Today.tips: The downward pointing represents the present moment.

Tomorrow.description: Touch the chin with the thumb of a closed hand, then move the hand forward in an arc.
Tomorrow.tips: The forward motion represents moving into the future.

Yesterday.description: Touch the chin or cheek with the thumb, then move the hand backward over the shoulder.
Yesterday.tips: The backward motion represents looking back in time.

One.description: Hold up the index finger, palm facing forward.
One.tips: Keep only the index finger extended, all others curled.

Two.description: Hold up the index and middle fingers in a V shape, palm facing forward.
Two.tips: Like a peace sign facing forward.

Three.description: Hold up the index, middle, and ring fingers, palm facing forward.
Three.tips: Three fingers extended with clear spacing.

Four.description: Hold up four fingers (index through pinky), thumb curled across the palm.
Four.tips: Keep the thumb tucked, all four fingers extended.

Five.description: Hold up all five fingers spread apart, palm facing forward.
Five.tips: All fingers and thumb spread wide and visible.

Six.description: Extend the thumb and pinky finger, with the middle three fingers curled down. In ISL, six can vary by region.
Six.tips: Regional variations exist — this is one common form.

Seven.description: Touch the thumb to the ring finger while extending the index, middle, and pinky fingers.
Seven.tips: The specific finger configuration distinguishes seven from other numbers.

Eight.description: Touch the thumb to the middle finger while extending the index, ring, and pinky fingers.
Eight.tips: Similar to seven but with a different finger touched.

Nine.description: Touch the thumb to the index finger while extending the middle, ring, and pinky fingers.
Nine.tips: The thumb-index touch creates the nine configuration.

Ten.description: Hold up both hands with all fingers extended and shake them slightly, or show a closed fist with thumb up twice.
Ten.tips: Ten is represented by showing all ten fingers or a specific regional sign.

Love.description: Cross both arms over your chest in an X shape, fists closed, hugging yourself.
Love.tips: The self-hug clearly represents love and affection.

Happy.description: Place both flat hands on your chest and brush them upward and outward repeatedly with a smile.
Happy.tips: The upward motion represents joy rising from the heart.

Sad.description: Place both hands on your face and draw them downward slowly with a sad expression.
Sad.tips: The downward motion and facial expression together convey sadness.

Angry.description: Make claw-like hands in front of your face and pull them down firmly with a tense expression.
Angry.tips: The tension in the hands and face shows anger.

Scared.description: Hold both fists near your chest, then quickly open them while pulling the hands back, as if startled.
Scared.tips: The sudden opening and pulling back represents being startled.

Tired.description: Place both bent hands (fingertips touching) on your chest, then let them fall/drop downward.
Tired.tips: The dropping motion represents energy draining away.

Cold.description: Hug yourself with both arms and shiver/shake your body slightly.
Cold.tips: The shivering motion is the key element.

Hot.description: Hold your hand near your mouth with fingers spread, then quickly pull it away and shake it, as if touching something hot.
Hot.tips: The pulling away motion represents reacting to heat.

Great.description: Hold both hands up with thumbs extended, then thrust them forward with enthusiasm.
Great.tips: The forward thrust with thumbs up shows enthusiasm.

Bad.description: Touch your chin or lips with your flat hand, then flip the hand downward palm-down.
